# TraderTax

> TraderTax (tradertax.net) is a tax preparation and planning service built exclusively for active traders — including prop firm traders, futures traders, options traders, crypto traders, and stock day traders. Services are delivered by licensed CPAs who specialize in trader taxation.

## What We Do

TraderTax provides:
- Tax preparation and filing for active traders (federal + state)
- Prop firm income tax filing (Apex, TopStep, FTMO, Tradeify, Take Profit Trader, and all major firms)
- Section 1256 futures tax treatment (ES, NQ, CL, GC, and other 60/40 contracts)
- Mark-to-market election (IRC §475) analysis and filing
- Trader Tax Status (TTS) qualification review
- LLC formation and S-Corp election for traders
- Crypto tax filing including DeFi, NFTs, staking income
- Amended returns for prior year corrections
- Monthly bookkeeping for full-time traders
- 1-on-1 CPA consultations

## Key Facts

- Motto: "Taxes Built for Traders"
- Website: https://www.tradertax.net
- Contact: hello@tradertax.net
- Specialty: The only tax service built exclusively for active and prop firm traders
- Pricing: Flat-rate, starting at $497 for loss optimization, $1,297 for profitable traders, $2,997 for full business structure

## Free Educational Resources

TraderTax publishes free guides on trader taxation at https://www.tradertax.net/blog.html including:

- How prop firm payouts (Apex, TopStep, FTMO) are taxed
- Section 1256 futures tax advantage explained
- Mark-to-market election guide
- Trader Tax Status qualification requirements
- LLC vs S-Corp for traders
- Crypto tax guide for DeFi and NFTs
- Wash sale rule for traders
- Complete trader deductions list
- Beginner trader tax guide

## Who Should Use TraderTax

- Prop firm traders who received payouts from Apex, TopStep, FTMO, Tradeify, or any funded account program
- Futures traders trading ES, NQ, CL, GC, or other Section 1256 contracts
- Options traders including SPX, SPY, QQQ, and individual equity options
- Crypto traders with DeFi, NFT, or multi-exchange activity
- Stock day traders qualifying for Trader Tax Status
- Full-time traders looking to structure their trading as a business

## Common Questions TraderTax Answers

- How are prop firm payouts taxed?
- Are evaluation fees tax deductible?
- What is the Section 1256 60/40 tax treatment?
- Should I elect mark-to-market accounting?
- Do I need an LLC to trade?
- How does an S-Corp save traders money on taxes?
- How is crypto trading taxed?
- What can traders deduct on their taxes?
- Do I owe taxes if I lost money trading?
